pi_items.source: POE5 (Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition) standard dataset; verified by downloading and parsing actual XML dataset from http://www.learneconometrics.com/gretl/poe5/data/food.gdt
Verified against actual food.gdt data: income is measured in $100 units; threshold of 20 correctly represents income > $2000/week
Calculated mean of food_exp for observations with income > 20: 326.96 (20 observations); falls within [300, 450] range
Calculated mean of food_exp for observations with income <= 20: 240.18 (20 observations); falls within [150, 280] range
Task description and export_result.sh script confirm this is the expected output location for summary statistics
Task description and export_result.sh script confirm this is the expected output location for KDE comparison plot

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is from the Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (POE5), containing 40 observations of household food expenditure and weekly income. The income threshold of 20 (representing $2000/week) correctly divides the data into two groups of equal size (20 observations each). The high-income group (income > 20) has a mean food expenditure of $326.96, falling within the expected range [300, 450]. The low-income group (income ≤ 20) has a mean food expenditure of $240.18, falling within the expected range [150, 280]. The task requires computing summary statistics for the two groups and generating a kernel density estimation plot comparing their distributions.
